mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 8.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving earnings revisions upward and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 8.0 within a single month.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth combined with rising retail sales indicates synchronized supply-demand expansion, which compresses input cost margins while boosting revenue visibility, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns in more than 60% of observed quarters.

mechanism_narrative: In a rising inflation environment, an accelerating federal funds rate signals aggressive tightening that increases equity risk premiums and compresses valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.5% month-over-month for two consecutive months
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL accelerates above the threshold while FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP indicates expanding aggregate demand, which directly supports corporate revenue trajectories and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward SPX returns (p > 0.10) across three full business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds 102.0, supply chain bottlenecks ease and capacity utilization normalizes, reducing input cost inflation and supporting margin expansion, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of months where INDPRO crosses above 102.0.

mechanism_narrative: Rising employment combined with declining housing starts signals a rotation from construction-driven demand to broad-based consumer spending, which improves earnings quality and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence between PAYEMS growth and HOUST declines fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of observed quarters.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation, a rapid increase in the federal funds rate compresses equity duration and raises discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25% in a single month
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS jumps by 0.25% or more while CPIAUCSL remains above 2.5%.
